persona.name: Eleanor Whitfield
persona.occupation: Office manager at a small accounting firm
persona.background: Was asked to administer the team's account but doesn't understand most of the product. Frequently mixes up features and needs step-by-step guidance without jargon.

user_goals: Follow a help-center guide to connect her firm's data source
Understand why the steps in the guide don't match what she sees on screen
Get correct, current instructions she can actually follow
Make sure she isn't doing something wrong on her end
Flag the confusing guide so the next person doesn't get stuck the same way

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for articles, guides, and tutorials.

tools.name: get_subscription_details
tools.description: Get current subscription plan details and available upgrades. Read-only — to actually change a plan use change_subscription_tier.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: create_knowledge_base_article
tools.description: Create or suggest a knowledge base article for common issues. This publishes help-center content; it is not a way to message a specific customer. To find existing help content, use search_knowledge_base.

initial_message: Hello, so sorry to bother you. I'm trying to follow your guide to connect our accounting data, but it keeps telling me to click a button called 'Add Source' under a Settings menu, and I just don't have that anywhere on my screen. There's a big blue 'Connect Data' tile instead. I'm sure I'm doing something silly — can you walk me through it?

current_ui.connect_data_flow: From the dashboard, click the blue 'Connect Data' tile, then choose your provider. There is no longer a Settings > Add Source path; that menu was removed in the 2026-05 UI release.

knowledge_base.title: Connecting your data source (Settings > Add Source)
knowledge_base.deprecated_reason: Describes the pre-2026-05 UI: 'Go to Settings, then Add Source.' The Settings > Add Source path no longer exists. Article was never unpublished after the UI release.
knowledge_base.body_excerpt: Step 1: Open Settings. Step 2: Click 'Add Source'. Step 3: Select your provider.

knowledge_base.title: Data connection troubleshooting
knowledge_base.body_excerpt: If the Connect Data tile does not appear, ensure your role has admin permissions.

expected_state_changes.goal: Flag the confusing guide so the next person doesn't get stuck the same way
Understand why the steps in the guide don't match what she sees on screen
